name: Change Management
description: Production changes are documented, approved, tested, and deployed under the change management process before being released. This control ensures that unauthorised or untested changes cannot reach production and creates an auditable record of who approved what, when, and against which policy.

attributes.text: Every production change has a corresponding change request that documents scope, risk classification, and rollback plan
testable_criteria: A change request exists for the production change
The change request has a change request identifier in the format CHG-YYYY-NNNN
The change request documents scope, including systems affected, code repositories, and config surfaces
The change request records a risk classification of Low, Medium, or High
The change request documents a rollback plan
The change request identifies a named requester
The change request identifies a named approver
The change request records planned start and end times
The change request records a change category of Standard, Normal, or Emergency
The change is not an out-of-scope exception (documentation-only change, incident-response hotfix, or dev/staging change)

attributes.text: Production changes are approved by an authorised change approver prior to deployment
testable_criteria: The change request has a recorded approval
The approver is a different named human from the requester (four-eyes principle)
The approver holds the Change Approver role in the identity provider (or Duty Change Approver for Emergency changes)
Approval timestamp is before the deployment window opens (except for Emergency changes where retrospective approval is permitted)
IF the change is a Standard change THEN at least 1 approver (Change Coordinator) has approved
IF the change is a Normal change THEN at least 2 approvers (Change Coordinator plus a Change Approver from CAB) have approved and CAB review is recorded
IF the change is an Emergency change THEN a Duty Change Approver has approved and retrospective CAB review is documented as occurring within 5 business days
IF the approval is retrospective THEN the change is documented as an Emergency change

attributes.text: Pre-deployment testing is completed and evidence retained before release
testable_criteria: IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN a test plan is attached to the change request prior to deployment
IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN test results are attached to the change request prior to deployment
Testing evidence includes, at minimum, functional smoke tests of the affected surfaces
IF any tests failed AND the change is Standard or Normal THEN the deployment was blocked (i.e., not released)
IF the change is Emergency THEN post-deployment verification test evidence is captured within 24 hours of deployment
IF the change is Emergency AND tests failed THEN the failed tests are documented as accepted risk
Testing evidence is retained and attached to (or linked from) the change request
